Title: N6-methyladenosine RNA modification regulates strawberry fruit ripening in an ABA-dependent manner

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: mRNA does not significantly increase at ripening initiation despite m6A hypermethylation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit ripening stages examined; translation efficiency regulated by m6A
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit ripening initiation; MTA RNAi or overexpression; actinomycin D and polysome assays
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential regulator of non-climacteric strawberry fruit ripening
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter (for transient expression constructs)
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: CDS-region m6A hypermethylation at ripening initiation; MTA binds transcript; m6A enhances translation efficiency rather than mRNA stability
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA signaling during strawberry ripening; m6A increases ABAR translation efficiency
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: m6A-seq; m6A-IP-qPCR; qRT-PCR; SELECT; site-directed mutagenesis; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; translation efficiency assay; RIP; MTA RNAi/overexpression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABAR transcript acquires ripening-associated m6A hypermethylation in the CDS region. 2. Unlike NCED5 and AREB1, ABAR mRNA abundance does not markedly increase at ripening initiation. 3. Mutation of the validated m6A site reduces translation efficiency without significantly affecting mRNA stability. 4. MTA directly binds ABAR transcript and promotes its m6A enrichment and translation, supporting ABA signaling during ripening.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune ABA sensitivity and ripening through translational control rather than transcript abundance
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ABA receptor perturbation may influence stress and developmental responses beyond fruit ripening

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ABA-responsive element-binding protein; bZIP-type transcription factor
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: increases at ripening initiation (S6 to RS1)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher transcript level at ripening initiation in fruit
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit ripening initiation; MTA RNAi or overexpression; actinomycin D assay
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential regulator of non-climacteric strawberry fruit ripening
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter (for transient expression constructs)
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: CDS-region m6A hypermethylation at ripening initiation; MTA binds transcript; m6A increases mRNA stability
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA-responsive ripening signaling; m6A stabilizes AREB1 mRNA
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: m6A-seq; m6A-IP-qPCR; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; SELECT; site-directed mutagenesis; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; mRNA stability assay; RIP; MTA RNAi/overexpression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AREB1 transcript exhibits ripening-associated CDS m6A hypermethylation. 2. AREB1 expression increases at ripening initiation. 3. Mutation of the specific m6A site accelerates mRNA degradation, indicating m6A-dependent transcript stabilization. 4. MTA directly binds AREB1 transcript and positively regulates its m6A enrichment and abundance, supporting ABA signaling in ripening.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential node for modulating ABA-dependent ripening through post-transcriptional control of a central ABA-responsive factor
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ABA signaling manipulation may affect broader stress-response transcriptional programs

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ripening initiation; MTA RNAi or overexpression
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: CDS-region m6A hypermethylation; MTA binding stabilizes transcript
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indirectly supports ripening-related metabolism by increasing translational capacity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: m6A-seq; RNA-seq; RIP; m6A-IP-qPCR; qRT-PCR; mRNA stability assay in MTA RNAi/OE fruit
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EIF2 transcript is hypermethylated by m6A in the CDS at ripening initiation. 2. MTA binds EIF2 transcript. 3. MTA positively affects EIF2 m6A enrichment, mRNA abundance, and stability. 4. This suggests an indirect route by which MTA can influence translation of many ripening-related genes.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: System-level target for modifying translational capacity, though likely too pleiotropic for precise breeding
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad effects on global translation likely cause pleiotropy
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Indirect regulator of nutrient metabolism via translation machinery

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ripening initiation; MTA RNAi or overexpression
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: CDS-region m6A hypermethylation; MTA binding stabilizes transcript
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indirectly supports ripening-related metabolism by increasing translational capacity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: m6A-seq; RNA-seq; RIP; m6A-IP-qPCR; qRT-PCR; mRNA stability assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EIF2B transcript acquires CDS m6A hypermethylation at ripening initiation. 2. MTA binds EIF2B transcript. 3. MTA increases EIF2B m6A enrichment, abundance, and stability. 4. This supports an indirect mechanism for MTA-mediated enhancement of translation during ripening.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential systems target but with likely broad pleiotropic effects
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely broad translational and developmental pleiotropy
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied as part of translation machinery impacted by MTA

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ripening initiation; MTA RNAi or overexpression
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: CDS-region m6A hypermethylation; MTA binding stabilizes transcript
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indirectly supports ripening-related metabolism by increasing translational capacity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: m6A-seq; RNA-seq; RIP; m6A-IP-qPCR; qRT-PCR; mRNA stability assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EIF3A transcript shows ripening-associated CDS m6A hypermethylation. 2. MTA directly associates with EIF3A transcript. 3. MTA promotes EIF3A m6A enrichment, abundance, and stability. 4. EIF3A is part of the indirect translational control route downstream of MTA.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential systems-level engineering target, with caution due to broad translational role
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic due to central role in translation initiation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports indirect effect of MTA on non-m6A-modified ripening genes

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ripening initiation; MTA RNAi or overexpression
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: CDS-region m6A hypermethylation; MTA binding stabilizes transcript
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indirectly supports ripening-related metabolism by increasing translational capacity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: m6A-seq; RNA-seq; RIP; m6A-IP-qPCR; qRT-PCR; mRNA stability assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EIF3C transcript exhibits CDS m6A hypermethylation at ripening initiation. 2. MTA binds EIF3C transcript. 3. MTA enhances EIF3C m6A enrichment, transcript abundance, and stability. 4. This supports indirect MTA control of translation during fruit ripening.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible systems-level target but high pleiotropy risk
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely broad pleiotropic impact on protein synthesis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Translation machinery target downstream of MTA

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ripening initiation; MTA RNAi or overexpression
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: CDS-region m6A hypermethylation; MTA binding stabilizes transcript
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Indirectly supports ripening-related metabolism by increasing translational elongation capacity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: m6A-seq; RNA-seq; RIP; m6A-IP-qPCR; qRT-PCR; mRNA stability assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EF1A transcript shows CDS m6A hypermethylation during ripening initiation. 2. MTA directly binds EF1A transcript. 3. MTA promotes EF1A m6A enrichment, transcript abundance, and stability. 4. EF1A likely contributes to the indirect enhancement of translation of ripening-related genes.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential systems target but likely unsuitable for precise trait engineering due to broad translational role
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High pleiotropy risk because elongation factor affects general protein synthesis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Provides mechanistic support for indirect translation regulation by MTA

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Carotenoid cleavage branch leading to ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: increases at ripening initiation (S6 to RS1) in fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: increases at ripening initiation in strawberry fruit
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit ripening initiation stage; MTA RNAi or overexpression; actinomycin D in mRNA stability assay
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential regulator of non-climacteric strawberry fruit ripening
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cleaves 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids to produce xanthoxin, a precursor for ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as rate-limiting enzyme for ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA biosynthesis and thereby supports strawberry fruit ripening; MTA-mediated m6A stabilizes its mRNA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: m6A-seq; m6A-IP-qPCR; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; SELECT site validation; site-directed mutagenesis; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; mRNA stability assay; RIP with MTA; MTA RNAi/overexpression in strawberry
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter (for transient expression constructs)
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: m6A hypermethylation in CDS at ripening initiation; MTA directly binds transcript; m6A increases mRNA stability
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: m6A-seq (GSE167183; GSE174703); RNA-seq (input from m6A-seq, accession linked to study)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NCED5 transcript shows ripening-associated m6A hypermethylation in the CDS region. 2. NCED5 mRNA abundance increases at ripening initiation. 3. Mutation of the validated m6A site accelerates NCED5 mRNA decay, showing m6A-dependent stabilization. 4. MTA binds NCED5 transcript and positively regulates its m6A enrichment and abundance, linking ABA biosynthesis to ripening.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to modulate ABA level and ripening timing in strawberry; could be combined with m6A regulators such as MTA for ripening control
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ABA pathway manipulation may alter broader development and stress responses; specific tradeoffs not directly quantified
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene-metabolite effect is mediated post-transcriptionally through m6A, not only transcriptional regulation

Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus and cytoplasm; colocalizes with MTB in nucleus
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: increases significantly at ripening initiation (S6 to RS1)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: upregulated during ripening initiation in diploid and cultivated strawberry fruit
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit ripening initiation; RNAi and overexpression in fruit
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential regulator of non-climacteric strawberry fruit ripening
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: post-transcriptional control of ABA biosynthesis/signaling gene expression and broader ripening-related translation capacity through m6A methylation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: NCED5; ABAR; AREB1; EIF2; EIF2B; EIF3A; EIF3C; EF1A
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes m6A deposition on target transcripts; stabilizes NCED5, AREB1, EIF2, EIF2B, EIF3A, EIF3C, and EF1A mRNAs; enhances ABAR translation efficiency
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: RNA methylation-mediated post-transcriptional activation via mRNA stabilization and promotion of translation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with MTB as putative methyltransferase heterodimer
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MTB; NCED5 transcript; ABAR transcript; AREB1 transcript; EIF2 transcript; EIF2B transcript; EIF3A transcript; EIF3C transcript; EF1A transcript
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Indispensable positive regulator of strawberry ripening by targeting ABA pathway transcripts and global m6A methylation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA-dependent strawberry fruit ripening; RNAi delays ripening and OE accelerates ripening
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi and overexpression in strawberry fruit; LC-MS/MS; RIP; m6A-seq; m6A-IP-qPCR; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; mRNA stability assay; translation efficiency assay; Y2H; split luciferase complementation; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: MTA is itself an epitranscriptomic writer; altered MTA expression changes global m6A methylation landscape
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: m6A-seq (GSE167183; GSE174703); RNA-seq; LC-MS/MS global m6A quantification
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MTA expression rises at ripening initiation in strawberry fruit. 2. MTA interacts with MTB and is a core m6A methyltransferase component. 3. MTA RNAi lowers global m6A and delays ripening, whereas overexpression raises m6A and accelerates ripening. 4. MTA binds ABA pathway transcripts NCED5, ABAR, and AREB1 and enhances their m6A status, stability, or translation. 5. MTA also stabilizes transcripts encoding translation initiation and elongation factors, extending its impact beyond direct ABA targets.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for engineering ripening timing and fruit quality via epitranscriptome manipulation, but requires caution because it affects global m6A and many ripening-related pathways
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad perturbation of global m6A may cause pleiotropic effects across development, secondary metabolism, cell wall processes, and hormone responses
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts analogously to METTL3-like catalytic subunit; direct mechanistic link established between m6A deposition and ABA-pathway-mediated ripening

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: RNA m6A methyltransferase cofactor/core complex component
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: increases significantly at ripening initiation (S6 to RS1)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: upregulated during ripening initiation in diploid and cultivated strawberry fruit
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit ripening initiation; RNAi and overexpression in fruit
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential regulator of non-climacteric strawberry fruit ripening
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: m6A methyltransferase complex function supporting ABA-pathway-mediated ripening
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: supports m6A methyltransferase activity as MTA-interacting partner
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Necessary positive regulator of normal strawberry ripening as part of the m6A writer complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strawberry fruit ripening; RNAi delays and OE accelerates ripening
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi and overexpression in strawberry fruit; qRT-PCR; Y2H; split luciferase complementation; subcellular colocalization
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Functions as part of the epitranscriptomic writer machinery for m6A
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq expression profiling in fruit stages
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MTB is the strawberry homolog of METTL14/Arabidopsis MTB and contains an MT-A70 domain. 2. MTB expression increases during ripening initiation. 3. MTB physically interacts with MTA and colocalizes with MTA in the nucleus. 4. MTB RNAi delays ripening and overexpression accelerates ripening, indicating that MTB is required for normal fruit ripening.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for manipulating ripening through the m6A writer complex, likely best used together with understanding of MTA-centered network
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic effects due to broad role in RNA methylation; specific metabolic tradeoffs not quantified
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study provides stronger direct downstream target evidence for MTA than for MTB
